entries.session_topic: Zero Onboarding — Reconciliation against v2.4 Integration Guide + Email Thread + Three-Frame Reframe
tasks: Read the v2.4 SP Integration Guide (132 pages) — the technical-contract layer underneath the v1.1 business-rules guidelines

Read the email thread on the initiative — sponsor is Ghalib Al Khalafi (TDRA Digital Adoption Specialist, Development Department); meeting calendar: Apr 22 first meeting (done), Apr 28 fileshare-team meeting (today), Apr 30 Thursday management wrap-up

Critical new architectural finding: v2.4 §6 specifies that the QR is HMACSHA256-signed using the SP's shared secret. A 'static QR provided by DV team' as described in the v1.1 Zero Onboarding deck cannot follow the v2.4 spec without DV holding the SP's keys
Critical new finding 2: v2.4 Appendix V states 'Partner onboarding is an assisted process and not a self-serviced one' — self-service onboarding would itself be a contract change

Critical new finding 3: v2.4 §5.1.1.2 defines an 'idn' attribute described in the change history as supporting 'DV only approach request changes' — but this is a feature within standard integration (SP still has DID, secrets, hosts Receive Presentation API), NOT a replacement for integration. Easy to confuse with Zero Onboarding

Reframed the proposal into three possible interpretations: Frame A (UC 4.4 — Lite tier; SP still gets DID, lighter SLA), Frame B (DV-mediated; DV holds proxy DID and signs on SP's behalf — breaks v2.4 partnerId semantics), Frame C (Guest journey extension; no SP cryptographic identity at all — requires new flow definition)
Recommended position: Frame A. Frames B and C should be off the table unless TDRA explicitly chooses to revise the v2.4 contract
Updated zero_onboarding_guidelines_reconciliation.md with the v2.4 findings, the email-thread context, and the three-frame analysis

Updated and simplified the TDRA discussion deck (now v3): slide 1 presents the three frames with v2.4/v1.1 consequences for each; slide 2 first question is now 'Which frame are we in?'; slide 3 pre-conditions table maps to the actual Apr 28 / Apr 30 meeting calendar from the email thread

Logged that the email I (Fadi) sent on Apr 27 was based on the v1 question set (pre-PDF-review) and is now superseded — a follow-up reply pointing the team at the updated framing should go out before Thursday's wrap-up

entries.session_topic: VC Explainer — section 6 envelope blur fix + QA harness
tasks: Removed `backdrop-filter: blur(2px)` from `.env-layer` — the four-layer signature envelope diagram in section 6 is now sharp by default in both themes

Added a `?qacompact=1` early-init query param to index.html that collapses hero `min-height: 100vh` and hides chrome (progress bar, side nav, custom cursor) so a tall headless render captures the full page in one shot
Made `_theme_light.html` forward the query string to `index.html` so QA params still apply when previewing the light theme

artifacts.name: vc-explainer/index.html
artifacts.description: Section 6 envelope diagram blur removed; ?qacompact=1 QA helper added

artifacts.name: vc-explainer/_theme_light.html
artifacts.description: Forwards query string to index.html so QA params survive the light-theme redirect

entries.notes: User report: 'section 6 the diagram is still blurred by default I can see the things happening but the diagram itself is blurred from inside out'. Root cause: the `.env-layer` rule had `backdrop-filter: blur(2px)` for a frosted-glass effect, which compounded across the four nested layers (each layer blurred its parent's content), producing the inside-out fuzziness. Removing the single property fixed it without losing the layered look (border colours and translucent gradients remain). Side-quest: built a reliable headless QA path. Earlier attempts to render section 6 via URL hash, iframe wrappers, or fetch+srcdoc all failed because Chrome's `--screenshot` captures pre-scroll. Final approach: ?qacompact=1 collapses the 100vh hero so a 1440×9500 single-shot render fits the whole page; PIL crops the relevant band. Cleaner than the iframe gymnastics.

entries.session_topic: TDRA briefing — 10-slide condense + new gamma-presentation-formatter agent + Gamma-ready paste file
tasks: Condensed the 12-slide TDRA briefing into a 10-slide version by merging slides 7+8 (ICP problem + fix) and slides 9+10 (UAE Verify + DV-vs-UAE-Verify comparison). Saved as `tdra_business_first_briefing/slides_for_gemma_10slides.md`.

Researched Gamma (gamma.app) input format end-to-end via WebSearch + WebFetch on Gamma's developer docs and help center. Verified findings: only `---` (three ASCII hyphens) triggers a card break — not `##`, not em-dashes; no HTML-comment / frontmatter directive syntax exists, so any meta-text in the body becomes slide content; meta-instructions live in a separate ≤500-char `additionalInstructions` field; textMode `preserve` keeps wording, `condense` summarises, `generate` rewrites; for N cards, emit N-1 separators + set `numCards = N`; long bullets and 3+-level nesting get flattened.

Built a new Claude Code agent `gamma-presentation-formatter` at `.claude/agents/gamma-presentation-formatter.md`. The agent encodes the verified Gamma rules, mandates a two-artefact output (Section A body + Section B ≤500-char instructions), strips TITLE/SUBTITLE/BODY/VISUAL HINT/SPEAKER NOTE scaffolding, runs a self-check on separator count, and advises on Gamma UI settings (paste mode + Preserve + numCards).

Followed the agent's own spec to convert the 10-slide source into a Gamma-ready paste file at `tdra_business_first_briefing/gamma_paste_ready_10slides.md`. Section A: 10 H2 cards with 9 verified `---` separators, no scaffolding leaks, italic placeholder lines on the two diagram slides (Triangle and Flow). Section B: 431 chars covering audience, tone, theme/palette, layout asks for slides 4/5/7/8/6.

Caught and fixed a structural-divider leak during self-check: an extra `---` between Section A and Section B (a markdown convention) would have inserted an 11th phantom card if the user copied the whole region. Replaced with an explicit `═══ END OF SECTION A — STOP COPYING HERE ═══` marker.

entries.notes: User had been calling Gamma 'Gemma' throughout the project; verified the workflow is gamma.app and proceeded on that assumption (flagged early in the session — no correction came back). Key insight from the research: most online tutorials get the slide-break syntax wrong (claim `##` works) — only `---` reliably breaks cards in paste mode. The new agent captures this so future sessions don't have to relearn it. The TDRA deck file is ready to paste directly; Gamma settings to use are explicit at the top (Paste mode + Preserve + 10 cards).

Walked through the 5-step chain: SP sharing request (HMACSHA256-signed QR per Integration Guide §6) → user consent → DV produces VP preserving ICP eSeal (CAdES/PAdES) → DV pushes to SP's Receive Presentation API → SP validates eSeal against ICP certificate chain

Flagged that the answer only holds for an integrated SP (Integration Guide Appendix V: 'Partner onboarding is an assisted process and not a self-serviced one'); non-integrated SPs cannot use the verification APIs at all — that's the Zero Onboarding Frame A/B/C question
Flagged the ICP eSeal Transition risk: SPs onboarding now must trust the new ICP HSM certificate chain, not just the legacy DDA-signed chain
